w:r.w:t: This document describes the MQTT communication protocol between the ESP32 fuel monitoring device and the web application. It covers all topics, payload schemas, configuration options, and includes a JavaScript quick-start example.
that measures fuel level inside a tank. It communicates over a cellular network (SIM7600 modem) to an MQTT broker, then your web application subscribes to receive real-time data.
Production note: The broker and credentials are configurable in firmware. Replace broker.hivemq.com with your private broker before deploying to production.
topic). The device only publishes if at least one of
has changed since the last send.
Fuel level as % of tank capacity (0–100)
Absolute fuel height in the tank
Calculated fuel volume based on tank geometry
Fuel temperature measured by the sensor
Cellular signal strength (negative; closer to 0 = better)
The sensor returns a raw 16-bit value (0–65535). The device converts it:
volume_l = calculated using configured tank geometry
Send a JSON message to the device’s
topic to update its settings. The device applies the configuration
and persists it to flash memory (survives reboot).
How often the device publishes telemetry (seconds)
When true, device publishes verbose logs to the
You can send only the fields you want to change. Omitted fields retain their current values:
Send an HTTP(S) URL pointing to a
firmware file. The device downloads and flashes it automatically, then reboots.
The device will verify the download before applying. If the download fails, the existing firmware remains active.
Failed to publish; payload saved to SD card
Could not connect to MQTT broker
Received malformed JSON on config topic
Could not write failed payload to SD card
string in the config payload selects the volume calculation algorithm.
Sections are stacked from bottom to top. Up to
When the cellular connection is unavailable:
Failed telemetry payloads are saved to the device’s
The on-disk payload wraps the original telemetry with a timestamp and error code.
When connectivity is restored, the device replays all saved payloads
Successfully retried files are deleted from the SD card.
Important for the web app: Retried messages may arrive out of chronological order relative to their ts field. Always use the ts field (not message arrival time) when ordering or displaying data.
const DEVICE_MAC = 'A4:CF:12:7E:3B:01'; // replace with your device MAC
// Or all devices in the project
// Set a cylindrical tank (radius 0.5 m, height 2 m)
Document generated from firmware source — Fuel level with SOJI SENSOR2
